situation: audit-2026-07-07-047 derived fix dispatch. extractJson in vision-adapter.ts used naive response.slice(jsonStart) to end-of-string then JSON.parse. When Vision API returns trailing prose after the JSON array/object, parse fails or extracts wrong substring. Also rejected SZ-BG-M-005 as duplicate (already fixed in prior audit cycle commit 5d3d756 SH2-M-003).
action: Replaced slice-to-end fallback with bracket-matching extractor (extractBalancedJson) that tracks depth of [/{ vs ]/} while respecting string literal boundaries. Only extracts balanced JSON substring. Falls through to error if no balanced match found.
outcome_evidence: Commit 47c1bc5 on auto/opt-background-1783213292 pushed to origin. Pre-existing test failure (ocr-slice.test.ts:125) confirmed unrelated — fails identically without this change (git stash test). All other 52 tests pass.
next_suggestion: Next pending fix: SZ-BG-M-007 (Vision API fetch calls have no timeout — can hang indefinitely). Then SZ-BG-M-008 (Non-PNG images skip all scaling/tiling/enhancement).

audit_findings.finding: extractJson slices from first [/{ to end of string then parses — when model response contains trailing text after valid JSON, parse either fails (throwing away valid data) or accidentally succeeds on a wrong substring if earlier pseudo-JSON exists
audit_findings.remediation: Replace with bracket-matching extraction that finds balanced closing bracket, respecting string literals. Only attempt JSON.parse on the balanced substring.
